title: Reading EXPLAIN ANALYZE

glossary.definition: The actual process of running a query or command within the database system.
Relating to tools or processes used to identify the cause of a problem, like finding why a query is slow.
The part of the database system that figures out the fastest and most efficient way to execute your query.

overview.what: EXPLAIN ANALYZE executes your query and returns a detailed execution plan showing exactly how the database retrieved the rows. It reveals whether it used an 'Index Scan', 'Seq Scan', or a specific JOIN algorithm.
overview.why: It is the primary diagnostic tool for SQL. Without it, tuning a query is just guesswork.

why_learning_this: Stop guessing why a query is slow and see the exact bottleneck.
Prove that a new index is actually being used by the database optimizer.

common_mistakes.title: Forgetting the ANALYZE keyword
common_mistakes.explanation: Using just EXPLAIN shows the database's *estimated* plan. Adding ANALYZE actually runs the query and shows *actual* execution times and row counts.

recall_questions.q: What does a 'Seq Scan' node in an EXPLAIN plan indicate?
recall_questions.answer: It indicates the database read the table sequentially from start to finish, rather than using an index.

understanding_checks.query: SELECT count(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'cancelled';
understanding_checks.question: What rows does this return?
understanding_checks.why: There is only one cancelled order (id 5, Diana). EXPLAIN ANALYZE on this query would show how many rows were scanned to find it.

understanding_checks.query: SELECT customer_id FROM orders WHERE order_date = '2023-04-02' ORDER BY id;
understanding_checks.question: What rows does this return?
understanding_checks.why: Order 3 is by Bob (id 2) on that date. The execution plan would reveal if it scanned all 8 orders or used an index on order_date.

query_walkthrough.query: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT name FROM customers WHERE country = 'US' ORDER BY id;
query_walkthrough.focus: The EXPLAIN output is returned as a set of text rows describing the execution plan, showing the Seq Scan or Index Scan.

practice_tasks.title: Check the Execution Plan
practice_tasks.prompt: Given this query, change it so that it returns the execution plan with actual runtimes instead of the query results.
practice_tasks.starter_code: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price < 50 ORDER BY id;
practice_tasks.solution: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM products WHERE price < 50 ORDER BY id;

challenge.title: Explain a Join
challenge.prompt: Write a query that returns the execution plan and actual run times for joining customers and orders where the customer_id is 1. (Select customer name and order status).
challenge.solution: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT c.name, o.status FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE c.id = 1 ORDER BY o.id;
